question: What is the location and function of the RRV (Reactor Recirculation Valve)?

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: This section describes the evaluation of the NuScale Power Plant’s system performance across its design power range. It outlines the criteria and parameters used to optimize plant operation, such as coolant temperatures and flow rates, and explains that calculations are performed using thermal hydraulic software and verified by hand. The analysis accounts for uncertainties and confirms that the system’s natural circulation design results in low pressure losses, with primary coolant flow unaffected by steam generator tube plugging.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components within the NuScale reactor coolant system, specifying alloys and standards for each part to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility. It references relevant sections of the BPVC (Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code) for material specifications and compliance.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for reactor components.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module's Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and emergency valves, and associated piping. It details how the steam generators are integrated into the reactor vessel, forming part of the primary and secondary pressure boundaries, and references other sections for specific component design bases.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain fuel integrity, minimize solid deposits, and ensure uniform boron concentration under all operating conditions, supporting both performance and safety requirements.
